prompt:
Goal: Create a cute Japanese perfume advertising flyer for an original tomato-cat fragrance, like a soft commercial poster for a kawaii cosmetic brand. Use TOMATONYAN as the perfume name and keep the overall feeling warm, sweet, and slightly premium.

Canvas: Wide horizontal flyer, 16:9 aspect ratio, divided into a large left hero advertisement panel and a narrower right information panel. Background is warm cream with peach tones, soft sunlight, shallow depth of field, blurred tomato vines and greenery around the edges, and a clean magazine-layout finish.

Main left hero panel: Feature exactly 2 main tomato-cat characters in the center-left area: 1 glossy red tomato cat sitting on a round glass perfume bottle, and 1 larger tomato cat wearing a pale cream hoodie with cat ears. Both characters have tomato stems as leafy hair, tiny black glossy eyes, small white mouths, blush cheeks, and cute rounded bodies. The perfume bottle is transparent glass with pale peach liquid, a tomato-shaped red cap/character head, and white branding text reading “TOMATONYAN EAU DE PARFUM” plus a tiny tomato icon. Add a dotted circular “NEW” badge on the lower left of the hero area with Japanese product/price text. Add a large handwritten-style Japanese headline near the upper left: トマトにゃん、はじめての香り。. Add smaller Japanese copy above and below it, including a cute heart. Near the bottom center of the hero area, add a short handwritten Japanese message beside tiny paw-print accents.

Bottom left brand strip: Add a full-width muted coral rectangle at the bottom of the left panel. Include a small tomato icon, the large serif brand text “TOMATONYAN”, smaller text “EAU DE PARFUM”, and two short Japanese tagline lines in white.

Right information panel layout: Arrange exactly 6 rounded rectangular cream cards on a pale peach background, separated by clean gutters. Use delicate red-brown serif English headings with tiny paw/flower ornaments and smaller Japanese subtitles. The 6 cards are: 1 “FRAGRANCE” scent notes card, 1 “POINT” recommendation card, 1 “DESIGN” design explanation card, 1 “ABOUT” character story card, 1 “HOW TO USE” usage card, and 1 message card from the tomato cat.

Card details and counts: The “FRAGRANCE” card contains exactly 3 scent note rows labeled TOP, MIDDLE, and LAST, each with a small ingredient icon: tomato for TOP, flowers for MIDDLE, and a pale citrus/wood-like icon for LAST. The “POINT” card contains exactly 4 recommendation rows with circular line icons: handbag, sparkle, heart, and gift. The “DESIGN” card contains a large product photo-style illustration of the tomato-cat perfume bottle plus exactly 2 small thumbnail images underneath/near it: one bottle thumbnail and one tomato-stem close-up thumbnail. The “ABOUT” card contains Japanese explanatory text and 1 small full-body tomato-cat in a cream hoodie at the bottom. The “HOW TO USE” card contains exactly 3 usage icons with Japanese captions: wrist/neck spray, hair/clothing scent, and after-bath relaxation. The message card contains Japanese message text and 1 small tomato-cat in a hoodie on the right.

Visual style: Combine watercolor-like kawaii character illustration with polished cosmetic product photography. Use glossy highlights on the red tomato surfaces and glass bottle, soft shadows on the table, warm beige paper texture, muted coral accents, and elegant brown typography. The characters should look adorable and rounded, with subtle droplets/highlights on the tomato skin. Keep the flyer clean, balanced, and commercial, as if advertising a limited-edition fragrance.

Constraints: English section headings may be used, but most body copy should appear as neat Japanese advertising text. Avoid real-world logos, barcodes, watermarks, or extra characters. Keep the exact visible item counts listed above. The overall palette should be cream, peach, tomato red, warm brown, and leafy green.
